title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
summary: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov presents a technical talk on building and scaling AI agents in production for government workflows. The session covers agent architecture patterns, evaluation methods, monitoring approaches, tool integration, and engineering practices learned from deploying agents across thousands of state and local governments.
writeup: Gabe De Mesa of OpenGov details how the company built and scaled OG Assist, an AI agent system deployed across thousands of state and local government workflows. The talk covers agent architecture patterns, evaluation methods, monitoring and observability, and tool integration, drawing on engineering practices from production deployment. It matters because it grounds agent design in the constraints of real government operations rather than demos, addressing concerns like long-context handling and the A2A protocol. Key takeaways center on evals and observability as production requirements and on patterns for integrating personal tools into agent workflows.
